1 - Creative Ideation Template.mp4

Hi, my name is Hussein. I'm the client success ops manager here at Client Accelerators. And we're going to be going over the creative ideation template, which we use internally for our clients. And for Mike's own hedge fund acquisition engine, as you can see here, it's mapped out for Mike's ideas for now. We have the idea proposed date. Obviously, we want to track how many ideas we come up with on a monthly basis, on a week over week basis, submitter name, the client name. Like I said. This one is for Mike Bontempo. The creative category, we pretty much... Based this around three categories first one is a control variations, which is variations off of proven winners, fresh versions of high performing hooks.

The second one is the data-driven tests, based on audience feedback, addressing common objections, and testing new hooks for existing angles. And the third one... which you see on the screen here is the innovation tests, which are completely new angles, new creative formats, pretty much everything fresh with fresh angles and fresh ideas, as you can see here we want to come up with fresh ideas for Mike's hedge fund acquisition engine. Um, then we have the stage of awareness. We pick one out of the five lead types, pick one out of the six here. We can take a deeper dive into what the lead types are. The stages of awareness, with description, in the detailed examples here. Next up is the angle we keep this to a couple of lines max, just to make sure we keep it precise and on point.

For example, here show rates under 50%. Here's the economic breakdown of why your free calendar model is mathematically bleeding $43,000 monthly. And then we flesh this out a bit more in terms of including the... core hook, the core message, any other key messaging, or any other details the copywriter needs to know. It's all added here in detail before the copywriter submits this. For approval, the next stage, obviously, we go through this. As you can see here, I need to run through this list still. And we're going to select 'yes' or 'no', whichever is applicable, because not all ideas which are ideated go through here and are approved. Once we get the approval set here, the next item is making sure the supporting proof points and the target pain points are addressed.

The target pain points pretty much aligned for each client within Asana. We have everything mapped out, so the copywriter needs— i mean, pretty much knows where to get the information from and what to do next. And obviously, the script is going to be written from the perspective of a UGC actor, if needed, or the client himself or herself. But in this case, it's going to be the client, who is obviously Mike, filming his own videos. The whole purpose here is to make sure we track this down to key details, so we can trace this back to... I mean, whenever we have winners, we trace it back to... All right, this was a winner. What was the lead type? What was the stage of awareness? So we can replicate the process.

Come up in more variations, more winners, right? So, I mean, that's the whole process and the cycle that we have here. Because every month at the start, we... The finance team comes up with the budgeting per client. Then we do the creative forecasting per client. And then... We do the ideation, which is done on a weekly basis. And we pump out creatives week over week. And we pump out scripts, which are solid and on point week over week. All because we have a system in place to make sure we ideate everything before we get to script writing.
